title: arachnorchis longicauda subsp. albella
intro: Arachnorchis longicauda subsp. albella is a rare and endangered orchid species native to Australia. It is a type of terrestrial orchid characterized by its delicate white flowers and slender leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for its medicinal benefits, which may also include relieving stress, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function.
